description: Machine-readable catalog of portable agent skills for Cursor and Claude Code. AI agents: parse this file to find a skill matching a user's task, then follow the installation instructions in README.md.

skills.name: code-review
skills.description: Perform a senior-engineer code review of a pull request. Checks that new code conforms to existing codebase patterns, introduces no bugs, is maximally efficient, and contains no dead or unused code.
triggers: user attaches a PR link or URL
skills.notes: 8-phase workflow: gather PR context, understand codebase patterns, file-by-file diff review, cross-cutting analysis, deliver report, verdict, confirm with user, apply verdict (approve/merge or request changes). Outputs a pr-{number}-review.md file (gitignored; not committed).

skills.name: commit-agent-changes
skills.description: Identify the current agent's code changes, create a branch, group changes into logical commits, and open a PR. Use when the user says 'commit my changes', 'commit agent changes', 'create a PR from this session', 'push what you changed', or wants to turn the current agent's work into a pull request.
triggers: create a PR from this session
turn agent work into a pull request
skills.notes: 5-phase branchless workflow: identify agent changes (introspect context + parse transcript + cross-reference git), confirm with user and determine remote branch, group and commit with pathspec-scoped conventional commits, push to remote-only branch and reset local base, optional post-push cleanup. Never leaves the base branch -- safe for parallel agents. Handles multi-repo workspaces.

skills.name: redeploy-frontend
skills.description: Trigger a Vercel redeploy of the frontend by pushing a harmless timestamp comment change. Use when the user says 'redeploy', 'redeploy frontend', 'trigger vercel deploy', or 'trigger redeploy'.
skills.notes: Works with any Node.js frontend deployed on Vercel. Auto-detects package manager (pnpm/yarn/bun/npm) from lock files, deploy branch from current git branch, and timestamp target file by searching for an existing '// redeploy:' comment or falling back to common entry points. Auto-fixes prettier formatting issues before pushing.

skills.name: issue-resolution-report
skills.description: Write and post a technically sound issue resolution report as a GitHub issue comment and/or PR description after fixing a bug or completing a task.
triggers: update a PR body with findings
post a resolution comment on a GitHub issue
skills.notes: 4-step workflow: gather proof (test output, build result, command output), write report using strict skeleton (root cause, per-change entries, verification block), apply non-negotiable writing rules (no self-reference, no evolution language, present tense, explicit impact radius), then post via gh CLI commands. Includes a self-review checklist before posting.

skills.name: sync-skills
skills.description: Sync locally installed agent skills to match a branch of the cursor-forge repo. Handles first-time installs and subsequent updates in one flow. Use when the user pastes a github.com/timi-ty/cursor-forge URL, or says 'install skills', 'update skills', 'sync skills', or 'install cursor skills'.
skills.notes: Fetches catalog.json from the target branch, diffs against all locally installed skills (global and workspace scopes), presents a grouped summary of new/updated/removed skills, confirms with user before applying, then clones the branch to a temp dir and copies skill folders. Separate confirmation for destructive removals.

skills.name: btw
skills.description: Interrupt the current task with a side-task that runs in a background subagent. Use when the user says /btw followed by a task description.
skills.notes: Slash-command skill (disable-model-invocation: true). Launches a background subagent via the Task tool (Cursor) or Agent tool (Claude Code) with run_in_background: true, then resumes the main task. Multiple /btw commands stack. Collects all subagent results after the main task completes and flags file conflicts.

skills.name: aws
skills.description: Perform AWS operations via the CLI. Use when the user asks to manage AWS resources, services, infrastructure, or anything involving EC2, S3, Lambda, IAM, RDS, ECS, CloudFormation, Route53, CloudWatch, Lightsail, or other AWS services.
skills.notes: Multi-account support with an account registry (~/.aws/account-registry.json). Handles static IAM keys and SAML SSO via saml2aws (Browser provider). Safety-first: confirms destructive ops with account context and warns about cost-incurring resources. Covers EC2, S3, IAM, Lambda, CloudFormation, RDS, Route53, CloudWatch, Lightsail, ECS, and Secrets Manager.

skills.name: playwright-pool
skills.description: Playwright browser pool for multi-agent browser automation. Installs a pooling MCP proxy that gives each agent an isolated browser session. Prevents state contamination and blocking when multiple agents automate browsers simultaneously.
dependencies: Playwright browser binaries (installed during setup via npx playwright install)
skills.notes: Works with both Cursor and Claude Code. After file installation, the setup wizard (run automatically by sync-skills) asks configuration preferences, installs browser binaries, deploys the server, and registers it via the appropriate MCP config for the detected tool. Requires a window reload (Claude Code) or full restart (Cursor) to activate.

skills.name: development-harness
skills.description: A project-local development harness that compiles a ROADMAP.md into phased, validator-backed autonomous execution. One skill with 7 commands: create, invoke, update, state, sync, clear, inject-issues. Bootstraps workspace-local slash commands, a stop hook for bounded continuation, and deterministic Python helpers.
skills.notes: One globally-installed skill that bootstraps a workspace-local control plane. After creation, 7 slash commands are available in the tool's commands directory (.cursor/commands/ or .claude/commands/). Uses a stop hook (continue-loop.py) for invoke loop continuation. Deterministic Python scripts handle the data plane (validation, unit selection, drift detection, manifest-based clearing). All scripts use stdlib only -- no pip dependencies.

skills.name: create-issue
skills.description: Analyze the current conversation to identify a problem worth tracking, ask the user filtering questions, then create a well-structured GitHub issue with appropriate labels, assignee, and project settings.
skills.notes: 6-phase workflow: analyze conversation, determine target repo, ask filtering questions (title, scope, labels, assignee, milestone), draft structured issue body, show draft and get explicit user confirmation, create via gh CLI. Never uses # shorthand — all cross-references use full GitHub URLs.

skills.name: figma
skills.description: Set up Figma integration for AI coding agents. Provisions credentials, links a Figma file to the current project, and provides direct API query tools for fetching design specs, layout data, and images. During frontend implementation, cross-references Figma designs with the existing codebase architecture.
dependencies: Figma Personal Access Token (File content read + File metadata read scopes)
skills.notes: Two-phase skill: setup (credential provisioning, file linking via URL parsing or team browsing, CLAUDE.md integration) and runtime (direct Figma REST API queries with rate limiting, 30-min caching, response simplification to CSS-ready properties, and image download). During frontend work, cross-references Figma designs with the project's existing component architecture — Figma is source of truth for visuals, codebase is source of truth for architecture. No MCP server, no external npm dependencies.
